channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Punjab achieves top national ranking in education with state-wide parent-teacher meetings
item.description: The Punjab government, led by Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh Mann, recently celebrated a landmark achievement as the state secured the No. 1 position in NITI Aayog’s School Education Quality Report 202...

item.title: Mick Jagger expresses hope for future Rolling Stones tour but rules out 2026 dates
item.description: Mick Jagger has said he is eager for The Rolling Stones to return to touring, though he made clear that fans should not expect any live dates in 2026. Speaking on BBC Radio 2’s Tracks of My Years, the...

item.title: Oburu Oginga Calls on Luo Community to Strengthen ODM Membership and Voter Registration Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: ODM leader and Siaya Senator Oburu Oginga addressed a large political rally at Kirembe Ground in Kisumu on May 31, where he encouraged members of the Luo community to remain loyal to the Orange Democr...

item.title: Tshwane considers renaming Wonderboom Airport to reflect Ndebele heritage through public consultation
item.description: The City of Tshwane is preparing to begin a public consultation process regarding a proposed name change for Wonderboom National Airport, with the intention of honouring the Ndebele people historicall...

item.title: Rising fuel costs in the US linked to Iran conflict add $59 billion to household spending
item.description: A report cited by The Independent indicates that Americans have collectively spent around $59 billion more on fuel since the escalation of conflict involving Iran under President Donald Trump’s admini...

item.title: Power fault at NEK station deepens Karachi’s ongoing water crisis
item.description: Karachi is currently facing a significant water shortage of 54 million gallons per day (MGD) after a disruption in electricity supply to the North East Karachi (NEK) Pumping Station, according to the ...

item.title: Stakeholders raise alarm over flawed party primaries ahead of 2027 elections in Nigeria
item.description: Barely eight months to Nigeria’s 2027 general elections, political stakeholders and analysts are expressing serious concern over the way political parties are handling their internal processes, especi...

item.title: Election Commission launches enumeration phase of Special Intensive Revision in Odisha, Mizoram, Sikkim and Manipur
item.description: The Election Commission of India has commenced the enumeration phase of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in the states of Odisha, Mizoram, Sikkim and Manipur. This phase began o...

item.title: Fire Service Officer Urges Akwa Ibom Government to Equip Federal Fire Service for Faster Emergency Response
item.description: An official of the Federal Fire Service in Akwa Ibom State, Chief Superintendent Emmanuel Okon, don appeal to the state government under Governor Umo Eno to support the service with modern firefightin...

item.title: AFP clarifies no coordinated Chinese military exercises near Bajo de Masinloc
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) clarified on May 31, 2026, that there were no coordinated military drills conducted by the Chinese People’s Liberation Army Navy (PLA-N) or China Coast Guard ...

item.title: Inuit-led university in Nunangat aims to improve access to post-secondary education in the Arctic
item.description: The article explores how the planned Inuit Nunangat University, set to open in 2030 in Arviat, Nunavut, could transform access to higher education for Inuit students across Canada’s Arctic. For many I...

item.title: ACT Police investigate suspicious house fire in Richardson that left home uninhabitable
item.description: ACT Police are investigating a suspicious house fire that destroyed a property on Clift Crescent in Richardson overnight. Emergency services were called to the scene shortly before 3am on Sunday after...

item.title: Angeles City building collapse death toll rises to nine; search and clearing operations continue
item.description: The death toll from the building collapse in Barangay Balibago, Angeles City, Pampanga has risen to nine, according to the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) as of May 31, 2026. The incident occurred on ...

item.title: Rivers Police Strengthen Security Operations for Eid-el-Kabir and Children’s Day Celebrations
item.description: The Rivers State Police Command announced that it stepped up security operations across the state ahead of and during the Eid-el-Kabir and Children’s Day celebrations to ensure the safety of residents...

item.title: Gina Rinehart attends WA of the Year awards in disguise
item.description: Gina Rinehart, Australia's wealthiest individual, marked her seventh consecutive year at the top of the Australian Financial Review Rich List by attending the Western Australian of the Year awards inc...

item.title: Call for National Treasury probe into alleged irregular R70m Pretoria building lease process
item.description: A preliminary anti-corruption investigation within the Department of Public Works has raised serious concerns about a R70m lease agreement for office accommodation in Pretoria, prompting calls for an ...

item.title: Arsenal Hold Big Bus Parade in London to Mark Premier League Victory
item.description: Thousands of Arsenal fans jam-packed the streets of London as dem celebrate their first Premier League title since 2004. Fans dey everywhere, waving flags, chanting, and even climbing trees and traffi...

item.title: KMC Confirms Hill Park Land Remains Protected Amid Alleged Encroachments
item.description: Recently, controversy has emerged regarding alleged encroachments and constructions near Karachi's Hill Park, raising concerns about the effectiveness of KMC's land department. The KMC had issued a co...

item.title: Clashes between football supporters and riot police in Paris following the Champions League final
item.description: Football fans and riot police have clashed in Paris in the aftermath of the Champions League final, leading to public disorder in parts of the French capital. The incident unfolded as large crowds gat...

item.title: CBSE acknowledges and addresses cybersecurity flaws in Class 12 digital evaluation system amid student complaints
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has acknowledged the presence of cybersecurity vulnerabilities in its On-Screen Marking (OSM) system, specifically within the OnMark portal used for eva...
